sanskrit: कविं पुराणमनुशासितारमणोरणीयांसमनुस्मरेद्यः। सर्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूमादित्यवर्णं तमसः परस्तात्॥
transliteration: kaviṃ purāṇam anuśāsitāram aṇor aṇīyāṃsam anusmared yaḥ / sarvasya dhātāram acintya-rūpam āditya-varṇaṃ tamasaḥ parastāt
literal_meaning: One should constantly remember the all-knowing, primeval, controller, subtler than the atom, sustainer of all, of inconceivable form, sun-luminous, beyond darkness.
contextual_meaning: Krishna gives Arjuna a precise cognitive-affective anchor for meditation: a mental image of the Supreme as simultaneously omniscient, intimate, and trans-luminous—an antidote to battlefield overwhelm by shifting attention from imminent chaos to an internalized, benevolent, hyper-coherent presence.
psychological_state: Shift from existential threat to self-transcendent absorption; a controlled dissociation that re-anchors identity in a non-material, benevolent source.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon about to operate on a loved one repeats an internal mantra of an all-knowing light smaller than a cell yet holding the entire OR, steadying her hands and calming her amygdala.
anchor_text: Hold the luminous, all-knowing presence that sustains you, closer than breath, beyond all fear.
